purpose: The commit fixes a queuing issue in the HCI (Host Controller Interface) for Bluetooth when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set. This flag indicates that the HCI device is being unregistered, and the commit ensures that hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error in this case to prevent potential use-after-free (UAF) issues.
The commit removes an unnecessary include statement for asm/termios.h in the n_hdlc.c file, as linux/tty.h already includes linux/termios.h.
The commit updates documentation for a store metric 'N/A' in the perf c2c tool. It reflects changes in the report table.
The commit fixes issues with vma lock handling during split vma and range unmapping, addressing race conditions and ensuring proper synchronization.
The commit adds documentation for the rt5682s codec, specifically for its AVDD and MICVDD supplies.
The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding a missing iounmap() call.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct, which is a minor code cleanup.
The commit changes the type of a struct member 'key_idx' from unsigned to signed to avoid warnings from static checkers.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs, ensuring consistency with savedefconfig output. This change does not affect functionality.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to replace an open-coded check for CAN-FD mode in the mcp251xfd driver. This improves code readability and maintainability.
The commit modifies test behavior to skip when RTC is not present, improving test relevance.
The commit removes a redundant assignment to the variable 'bytes' in the echoaudio MIDI code. This change cleans up a clang scan build warning.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from RCU Tasks, allowing rcuscale to be built without RCU Tasks.
The commit removes duplicated text 'be split' in a header file spte.h related to KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine) x86/mmu.
The commit reverts a change to the clk table population in drm/amd/display due to issues with dcfclk states, preventing underflow.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU, enabling support for this specific hardware configuration.
The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp to make it consistent with other names in the DW PCIe core driver.
The commit fixes a format warning in the amd-xgbe driver by changing the format specifier to match the type of the argument.
The commit fixes a branch range check issue in MIPS, specifically handling negative offsets to avoid warnings.
The commit removes a case for HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD from SetHwReg8188EU() and directly calls a function instead.
The commit declares non-global symbols as static in the arm64 kernel to fix sparse warnings.
The commit removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings.
The commit allocates space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref in preparation for allowing drivers to change array sizes.
The commit adds support for new SoCs and PMICs in the socinfo driver.
The commit enhances the recognition of chip cuts for the rtl8xxxu wifi driver, allowing it to identify all possible chip versions.
The commit removes custom software enable count for PRCMU clocks, as the core already keeps a software enable count.
The commit aims to refactor and group #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage in the RISC-V architecture, improving code readability and maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to add functioning I2C nodes later.
The commit fixes comments and typos in the thermal driver code.

The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the igc driver when releasing software synchronization. It handles a case where the hardware semaphore cannot be acquired, which can occur if the hardware is surprise-removed.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, specifically changing the clocking style to be more contemporary.
The commit removes a code block that checks for IS_SWAPFILE, which is always defined. This suggests a code cleanup or optimization.
The commit implements GTP echo response functionality, which is a new feature for handling GTP messages.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where appropriate, improving the effectiveness of cpumask operations.

The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR, which was failing and causing a message to be displayed on the console. This change ensures that the host driver no longer tries to get TIM information from the firmware.
The commit adds support for a new platform configuration without an amplifier, specifically for the alc5682 headset codec.
The commit adds a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg requests for protocols that don't support it, ensuring compatibility and preventing potential errors.
The commit improves the loading of xeCJK.sty in kerneldoc-preamble to prevent errors on certain Linux distributions.
The commit allows 'case' macros in checkpatch.pl, enabling more flexible and complex case handling in kernel code.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers in drm/simpledrm, improving code sharing and architecture.
The commit adds a jpeg decoding worker interface to ensure that three HWs run in parallel in MT8195.
The commit removes pointless VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb() as their information is already covered by tracepoints or can be retrieved from scsi tracing.

The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in the drm/amd/display module. It allows the system to enter a low-power state when the display is not in use, which can help reduce power consumption.

The commit fixes a type mismatch issue in the hqd_destroy() callback function, which was causing a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure. It updates the function pointer prototype to match the reality, ensuring that the third parameter, reset_type, has the correct type of 'uint32_t'.
The commit corrects the 'resets' property name in the dt-bindings for pinctrl in stm32. It also adds a missing 'additionalProperties' configuration.
The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver for GCC 12. It silences the warning by changing how the compiler handles certain operations, without fixing the root cause.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the code.

The commit reorganizes and refines the io_uring code by splitting out fixed file installation and removal into the filetable code. It changes the helper functions to take a ctx instead of an io_kiocb, making the code more coherent and aligned with its purpose.
The commit updates device tree bindings to support separate nodes for G1 and G2 decoders, which are independent blocks sharing a power domain controller.
The commit removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and combines its contents into the IWL_DEVICE_BZ macro. This change simplifies the code by reducing the number of macros.
